attempt: Transfer the TICKET68 lesson: a coarse cyclic quotient is not decisive if a theorem-plausible refinement turns it into a DAG. The problem-specific task is to prove refinement completeness or expose a persistent refined cycle.

bounded_result.candidate_theorem: Every low-margin quotient cycle is either broken by an explicit margin-refinement DAG, or a compatible exceptional low-margin cycle persists beyond the cutoff budget.
bounded_result.counterexample_target: a compatible large-even branch that remains in a refined low-margin cycle after all current explicit corrections
bounded_result.frontier_rule: A finite cyclic quotient is not a proof obstruction by itself. It must survive theorem-plausible refinement and be compatible with an infinite object.

obstruction: This is a transferred theorem target, not a solution.
candidate_theorem: Every low-margin quotient cycle is either broken by an explicit margin-refinement DAG, or a compatible exceptional low-margin cycle persists beyond the cutoff budget.
next_experiment: Build the problem-specific refined frontier and test whether cycles persist after the added coordinates.
claim_boundary: No goldbach proof and no certified goldbach counterexample.
